Outlet Profile: EDITORIAL PROFILE Jefferson Public Radio (JPR) is a network of public radio stations in Southern Oregon and Northern California.  Flagship station, KSOR, was established in 1969 on the campus of Southern Oregon University (SOU) in Ashland, Oregon.  A regional public radio service, JPR reaches over 1 million potential listeners in a 60,000-square-mile area of Southern Oregon and Northern California via the largest translator network in public radio. The Jefferson Daily is JPR's own 30-minute regional news magazine, featuring stories from the region, audio essays and news commentary, reports from Sacramento and Salem, and in-depth discussions with the interesting people that define the State of Jefferson. The program airs weekdays at 4:30 on JPR's Classics & News Service, and repeats at 5:30 on the Rhythm & News Service.
